name: ProBit Global Crypto Exchange API
what_it_does: South Korean cryptocurrency exchange with REST and WebSocket API — enabling traders and developers to access 800+ spot trading pairs, order management, market data, account operations, and IEO (Initial Exchange Offering) participation, with OAUTH-based API authentication and WebSocket for real-time data.
use_cases: Accessing real-time market data via ProBit API for cryptocurrency price tracking and order book analysis
Executing spot trades via ProBit API for algorithmic trading and market-making strategies
Managing orders via ProBit API for trading bot order lifecycle and portfolio management
Fetching account balances via ProBit API for multi-exchange portfolio aggregation
Streaming trade data via ProBit WebSocket for real-time market data applications
not_for: US-based traders (ProBit Global restricts US access; ProBit US is separate)
best_when: A developer needs access to a broad range of altcoin trading pairs (800+) with a Korean exchange that participates actively in IEOs and new token listings.
avoid_when: You need top-tier liquidity, US market access, or institutional-grade infrastructure.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials grant (non-standard for an exchange). Client ID and Client Secret from API management page. Token endpoint: accounts.probit.com/token. Access token: Bearer in Authorization header. Token expiry: 60 seconds (very short — must refresh frequently). Scopes not explicitly listed — all trading permissions with client credentials. REST API at api.probit.com/api/exchange/v1/. WebSocket: wss://api.probit.com/api/exchange/v1/ws.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; trading fees: 0.2% standard maker/taker
pricing.paid_tiers: PROB token holders get fee discounts; VIP tiers by volume
pricing.notes: South Korean company (Seoul). Founded 2018. ProBit Global (international) vs ProBit US (US-compliant, separate platform). 800+ trading pairs — extensive altcoin coverage. PROB token: native exchange token for fee reduction. IEO Starter: platform for token launches. ProBit Exclusive: premium listing tier. OAuth2 with 60-second token expiry is unusual for exchange API — very aggressive token rotation required. WebSocket for real-time streaming. KYC required for full trading access. GDPR compliance for EU users.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OAuth2 client credentials (unusual for exchange). 60-SECOND token expiry (very aggressive). Bearer token. Korean company. ProBit Global ≠ ProBit US. WebSocket for real-time. KYC required.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET requests idempotent. Order placement: use client_order_id for idempotency. Market data reads idempotent. Token refresh: must handle concurrent refresh race conditions with 60-second expiry.
known_agent_gotchas: CRITICAL: OAuth2 access tokens expire in 60 SECONDS — implement aggressive token refresh logic
OAuth2 client credentials (not HMAC signature) — unusual authentication pattern for crypto exchange
ProBit Global and ProBit US are SEPARATE platforms with different APIs — confirm which you need
60-second token expiry creates race conditions in concurrent trading bots — use token refresh locks
US traders must use ProBit US (probit.us), not ProBit Global (probit.com)
KYC verification required for full trading access — basic account may have withdrawal limits

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. OAuth2 client credentials — client secret requires secure storage. 60-second token expiry forces frequent auth — reduces window for stolen token abuse. Korean company: Korean financial regulations apply. No scope granularity documentation — unclear what permissions are limited. KYC reduces fraud risk. IP whitelist recommended for trading bots. Client secret is the sensitive credential — treat as API key equivalent.
